command_transcript:
Command chat:
  new [prompt...]
  review
  linearize
  quit

Patches and file locks are triggered automatically when agents interact with the orchestrator.

work-leaf> new add vim like visual mode for both panes when I do v I can select the text in focused the panes same keystrokes of vim y Y for copy maiusc V line select block select with ctrl v block select

work-leaf> new when an user prompt start with / and is followed by something without whitespace that is a command for the agent; the orchestrator must send it to the selected backend agent and show that backend response
work-leaf> new when review process is done the patch agent chat must be highlighted and ask is this feature done with yes/no; yes closes it, typing again reopens it
user-3 reviewed by review-user-3: rounds=2 resolved=yes
user-2 reviewed by review-user-2: rounds=2 resolved=yes
user-1 reviewed by review-user-1: rounds=4 resolved=yes

The branch also contains older provisional work-leaf commits, but the linearization scope names only three reviewed commits, so I’m keeping the proposal limited to those.
Proposed linearization before rewriting history:

- Keep `d3dbf8609c366cddec71ae810dfb8e6008c85f30` as one final commit for user-3.

- Keep `00fa721ff68ad0662d6e0ebc074e4c5241b3459b` as one final commit for user-2.

I will not merge these three because they are related UI behaviors but distinct reviewed feature patches. I will also ignore the other provisional commits visible between `master` and `HEAD` because the prompt explicitly scopes this run to the three listed reviewed commits.

Please accept this solution or request changes before I rewrite history.

sessions.title: review add-vim-like-visual-mode-for-both-panes-when-i-do-v-i-can-select-the-text-in-foc
lines: codex: Codex session 019eaca6-26cb-7453-af97-04ae9c7c99ca
codex: Codex is working
@work-leaf read docs/architecture.md src/ui.rs src/terminal_app.rs src/ui_harness.rs tests/terminal_ui.rs tests/ui_harness.rs

I have the mediated bundle open and am reviewing the visual-mode implementation against the rendered UI path and harness coverage. I’m focusing on behavior introduced by the provisional commits, not pre-existing terminal UI behavior.

The main code path is `TerminalUi::handle_key` into `start_visual_selection`, `move_visual_cursor`, `selected_visual_text`, and `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer`. I’m also checking whether copied text matches what the user can actually see in the focused pane.

One important detail has emerged: visual selection coordinates are based on `String::lines()`, but rendering uses `tui::Paragraph::wrap`. I’m checking whether wrapped rows can be selected or copied correctly, because that affects both panes in normal narrow terminal sizes.
I’m also checking the terminal-byte adapters. The real app and harness both route byte `22` to the same `UiKey::Char('\u{16}')`, so Ctrl-V reaches `TerminalUi` consistently.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: wrapped pane content cannot be selected or highlighted beyond the first rendered row. `right_widget` renders the focused right pane with `Paragraph::wrap(Wrap { trim: false })` at `src/ui.rs:727-735`, while visual state is built from logical newline-delimited strings via `remember_visual_lines` / `visual_lines` at `src/ui.rs:922-924` and `src/ui.rs:1696-1697`. `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` then maps each selected logical line to a single terminal row with `y = 1 + range.row` and clamps columns to `inner_width` at `src/ui.rs:1009-1017`. For any long chat line that wraps in the terminal, `l` can move the visual cursor past the visible first row, and `y` can copy those characters through `selected_visual_text` at `src/ui.rs:901-919`, but the rendered selection never reaches the wrapped continuation rows. The user sees no highlight for text being selected/copied, so visual mode is incorrect for normal wrapped terminal output.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: wrapped pane content still cannot be selected or highlighted beyond the first rendered row. The refresh only adds `tests/terminal_ui.rs::visual_character_mode_highlights_wrapped_right_pane_rows`, which reproduces the issue; the rendering logic in `TerminalUi::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` is unchanged from the snapshot I reviewed. `right_widget` renders with `Paragraph::wrap(Wrap { trim: false })`, but selection lines come from newline-delimited `visual_lines`, then map each selected logical line to `y = 1 + range.row`. For a long chat line that wraps, copied text can extend into characters displayed on continuation rows, while the visual highlight cannot reach those rows.
@work-leaf read src/ui.rs tests/terminal_ui.rs

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1013`: the wrapped-row fix does not compile because `start` is still used in `for column in start..end`, but the patch removed the local `let start = ...` binding from `TerminalUi::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer`. This blocks `cargo clippy` and `cargo test` before any visual-mode behavior can be validated. The fix needs to define `start` from `range.start`, likely clamped against the selected line width, before iterating.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` now applies wrapped-row offsets to both panes, but only the right pane is rendered with `Paragraph::wrap` at `src/ui.rs:727-735`. The left pane is a `List` built from one item per `left_visual_lines` entry at `src/ui.rs:675-724` and `src/ui.rs:937-979`; long `files:` or link rows do not have matching wrapped visual rows. After any long left-pane detail row, selection highlighting is shifted down and no longer corresponds to the focused pane text.

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: the wrapped-offset calculation is O(n²) for multi-row selections. `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` calls `wrapped_rows_before` once per selected range, and `wrapped_rows_before` scans all previous lines at `src/ui.rs:1696-1707`; `selected_ranges` returns one range per selected row for line/block/multiline selections at `src/ui.rs:1546-1581`. This violates the repo rule to flag O(n²) complexity increases.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` applies wrapped-row offsets to both panes, but only the right pane is rendered with `Paragraph::wrap` at `src/ui.rs:727-735`. The left pane is a `List` built from one item per `left_visual_lines` entry at `src/ui.rs:675-724` and `src/ui.rs:937-979`; long `files:` or link rows do not have matching wrapped visual rows. After any long left-pane detail row, selection highlighting is shifted down and no longer corresponds to the focused pane text.

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: the wrapped-offset calculation is O(n²) for multi-row selections. `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` calls `wrapped_rows_before` once per selected range, and `wrapped_rows_before` scans all previous lines at `src/ui.rs:1696-1707`; `selected_ranges` returns one range per selected row for line/block/multiline selections at `src/ui.rs:1546-1581`. This violates the repo rule to flag O(n²) complexity increases.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1012`: the current patch does not compile. `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` calls `visual_selection_cell(selection.pane, range.row, column, pane_x, inner_width)`, but the helper signature added at `src/ui.rs:1693` requires `lines: &[String]` as the second argument.

- `src/ui.rs:1012`: even after adding the missing argument, left-pane selections would wrap horizontally even though the left pane is rendered as one `ListItem` per row. The helper returns pane-specific `(x, y)`, but `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` immediately shadows `x` with `pane_x + 1 + (column % inner_width)`, so for `PaneFocus::Left` selected columns past `inner_width` are painted back at the beginning of the same row instead of being clipped.

- `src/ui.rs:1012`: the O(n²) issue is worse in this version. `wrapped_rows_before` is now reached from `visual_selection_cell` inside the per-character loop, so selecting across long wrapped right-pane content scans previous lines once per selected character. This remains an algorithmic complexity regression introduced by the patch.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1012`: the wrapped selection mapping is still O(n²) or worse for right-pane selections. `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` calls `visual_selection_cell` once per selected character; that helper calls `wrapped_rows_before`, which scans previous lines and recomputes `wrapped_ranges`, and also calls `wrapped_position`, which recomputes `wrapped_ranges` for the current line. Selecting a large wrapped pane repeatedly rescans the same text per character. This is still an algorithmic complexity regression introduced by the patch and must be flagged under the repo review rules.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1012`: the wrapped selection mapping is still O(n²) or worse for right-pane selections. `TerminalUi::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` calls `visual_selection_cell` once per selected character; that helper calls `wrapped_rows_before` and `wrapped_position`, and both recompute `wrapped_ranges` from line text. For a long selected line, `wrapped_position` rebuilds the same ranges for every selected character, so selecting `n` characters can do `O(n²)` work. This remains an algorithmic complexity regression introduced by the patch and must be flagged under the repo review rules.
@work-leaf read src/ui.rs tests/terminal_ui.rs docs/architecture.md src/terminal_app.rs src/ui_harness.rs tests/ui_harness.rs

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1012`: the wrapped selection mapping is still O(n²) or worse for right-pane selections. `TerminalUi::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` calls `visual_selection_cell` once per selected character; that helper calls `wrapped_rows_before` and `wrapped_position`, and both recompute `wrapped_ranges` from line text. For a long selected line, `wrapped_position` rebuilds the same ranges for every selected character. This remains an algorithmic complexity regression introduced by the patch and must be flagged under the repo review rules.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1018`: the current patch does not compile. In `TerminalUi::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer`, the old `if y >= body_height.saturating_sub(1)` guard remains before the new `let y = ...` binding, so `y` is referenced before it exists. The later combined bounds check already handles the same condition.
@work-leaf read src/ui.rs

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1708`: left-pane visual selections can paint into the right pane for long left-pane rows. `visual_cell_map(PaneFocus::Left, ...)` maps every character column in the logical left row, and `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` uses that column to compute `x = pane_x + 1 + visual_column` with only a whole-buffer bounds check. Unlike the original `range.end.min(inner_width)` path, this no longer clips to the left pane’s inner width, so line/block selections over long `files:` or link rows from `left_visual_lines` can reverse-style cells past the left pane border and into the right pane.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1708`: left-pane visual selections can still paint into the right pane for long left-pane rows. `visual_cell_map(PaneFocus::Left, ...)` maps every character column in the logical left row, and `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` computes `x = pane_x + 1 + visual_column` with only a whole-buffer bounds check. That no longer clips to the left pane inner width, so line/block selections over long `files:` or link rows from `left_visual_lines` can reverse-style cells past the left pane border and into the right pane.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1708`: left-pane visual selections can still paint into the right pane for long left-pane rows. `visual_cell_map(PaneFocus::Left, ...)` maps every character column in the logical left row, and `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` computes `x = pane_x + 1 + visual_column` with only a whole-buffer bounds check. The new regression test in `tests/terminal_ui.rs::visual_line_mode_clips_left_pane_highlight_to_left_pane_width` covers the expected behavior, but the `src/ui.rs` diff only reformats the right-pane loop; there is still no clipping to the left pane inner width.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1708`: left-pane visual selections can still paint into the right pane for long left-pane rows. `visual_cell_map(PaneFocus::Left, ...)` maps every character column in the logical left row, and `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` computes `x = pane_x + 1 + visual_column` with only a whole-buffer bounds check. The updated test assertion in `tests/terminal_ui.rs::visual_line_mode_clips_left_pane_highlight_to_left_pane_width` now only checks that a reset appears before the right-pane border, but the implementation still lacks the clipping that existed before the cell-map rewrite.
NO_FINDINGS

sessions.title: review when-an-user-prompt-start-with-and-is-followed-by-something-without-whitespace-t
lines: codex: Codex session 019eaca9-0bfa-72e1-b417-305826a4c121
codex: Codex is working
@work-leaf read docs/architecture.md src/terminal_app.rs src/ui_harness.rs src/ui.rs src/workspace.rs tests/terminal_app.rs tests/ui_harness.rs tests/terminal_ui.rs
I’m reviewing the mediated bundle for the changed files first, then I’ll trace the prompt-submission paths and test coverage against the architecture guidance.

The main path is `TerminalAppCore::handle_input` in prompt mode, with the new branch going through `route_prompt_slash_command` before `execute_command_line`. I’m also checking the harness mirror because the repository treats `UiHarness` as the terminal state-machine oracle.

One inconsistency is standing out: the real terminal path routes `:/status` without executing `execute_command_line`, but the harness records the same line as `work-leaf> /status` before trying the route. I’m checking whether that creates a review-level finding or is just a fixture transcript artifact.

The docs were updated in `docs/architecture.md` for the resulting terminal behavior, and the implementation follows the documented dependency path: terminal adapter -> `WorkLeafController::send_message` -> `CommandChat::send_to_agent_streaming_with_ids`. I’m checking edge cases around selection and the command surface before finalizing the review.

FINDINGS

- `src/ui_harness.rs:164` records `work-leaf> /status` before checking `route_prompt_slash_command`, then `src/ui_harness.rs:258` records `user-1> /status`. The real terminal path in `src/terminal_app.rs:534` only calls `handle_command_line` when routing returns false, so `:/status` is not echoed as a Work Leaf command there. This makes `UiHarness` and the manual harness display a stale command transcript line for the new routed path; move the `work-leaf>` recording behind the route-false branch and assert the absence in `tests/ui_harness.rs:449`.

- The patch affects terminal chat and backend send/resume behavior: `src/terminal_app.rs:642` routes prompt-mode slash input to `WorkLeafController::send_message`, which reaches `src/workspace.rs:248` and `send_to_agent_streaming_with_ids`. The review materials show fake backend/fake Codex coverage (`tests/terminal_app.rs:72`, `tests/terminal_app.rs:97`) but no required real-agent verification scenario or documented pre-agent blocker. Per the repo’s Real Agent Verification rule, that is not ready for an agent-facing change.

FINDINGS

- Missing required real-agent verification for an agent-facing change. `src/terminal_app.rs:642` routes prompt-mode `/...` input to `WorkLeafController::send_message`, which reaches `src/workspace.rs:248` and then `CommandChat::send_to_agent_streaming_with_ids`. The refreshed harness fix covers the UI transcript mismatch, and `tests/terminal_app.rs` uses fake backend/fake Codex coverage, but the review scope still does not show the required real configured agent smoke scenario or a documented pre-agent blocker.

No pending patch to rebase on my side.

The current refresh also shows a likely compile issue in `src/ui.rs`: the new call to `visual_selection_cell(selection.pane, range.row, column, pane_x, inner_width)` does not match the shown function signature, which expects `lines: &[String]` as the second argument. That looks separate from my previous review finding unless it is part of the same updated patch scope.

No pending patch to rebase.

The `src/ui.rs` compile mismatch I noted is resolved by the refreshed call signature usage. My review finding remains the missing required real-agent verification for the agent-facing slash-command routing change.

No pending patch to rebase.

The added wrapped-right-pane visual selection test is separate from the slash-command routing review finding. My remaining review result is still the missing required real-agent verification for the agent-facing prompt slash routing change.
@work-leaf read src/terminal_app.rs src/ui_harness.rs src/ui.rs tests/terminal_app.rs tests/ui_harness.rs tests/terminal_ui.rs docs/architecture.md
NO_FINDINGS

FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1017`: `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` now checks `if y >= body_height.saturating_sub(1)` before `y` is declared. The later declaration is `let y = 1_u16.saturating_add(...)`, so this will not compile. Remove the early `if y >= ...` or move it after `let y` and keep the existing combined bounds check.
@work-leaf locks run target -- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

FINDINGS

- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` fails. `src/ui.rs:1738` leaves `wrapped_position` unused after the cell-map rewrite, and `src/ui.rs:1723` triggers `clippy::needless_range_loop` because the loop indexes `cells` by `column`. The patch is not ready until those warnings are removed.

FINDINGS

- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` still fails at `src/ui.rs:1723` with `clippy::needless_range_loop`. The new `visual_cell_map` right-pane loop iterates `for column in start..end.min(len)` and indexes `cells[column]`; rewrite it to iterate `cells.iter_mut().enumerate().take(end.min(len)).skip(start)` and assign through the mutable cell.

@work-leaf locks run target -- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings && cargo test --test terminal_ui visual_character_mode_highlights_wrapped_right_pane_rows -- --exact && cargo test --test ui_harness scripted_harness_prompt_slash_command_routes_to_selected_agent_chat -- --exact
@work-leaf locks run target -- cargo test --test terminal_ui visual_line_mode_clips_left_pane_highlight_to_left_pane_width -- --exact && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

FINDINGS

- `tests/terminal_ui.rs:142` now fails, and the failure exposes the underlying UI bug: left-pane visual selection still reverse-styles cells into the right pane. In the rendered frame, reverse video starts on the selected left-pane row and is not reset until after the right-pane content containing `NOLEAK`, so the highlight leaks past the left pane boundary. `src/ui.rs::visual_cell_map` maps every character in long left-pane visual lines, and `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` only bounds against the full buffer, not the left pane width. The left-pane mapping or application needs to clip selected columns to the pane’s inner width.

sessions.title: review when-review-process-is-done-the-patch-agent-chat-must-be-highlighted-and-ask-is
lines: codex: Codex session 019eaca1-5226-71d1-a948-92397103cc55
codex: Codex is working
@work-leaf read docs/architecture.md README.md src/terminal_app.rs src/ui_harness.rs src/workspace.rs tests/ui_harness.rs tests/workspace.rs
I’m going to inspect the orchestrator-provided bundle only, then ground the review in the touched files and symbols from that snapshot.

The relevant paths are `src/workspace.rs`, `src/terminal_app.rs`, `src/ui_harness.rs`, and their tests. I’ve found the new review-completion prompt constants and READY-state handling, so I’m reading those call paths carefully before deciding whether this patch is correct.

One edge I’m checking is whether all ways of sending text to a selected patch-agent chat go through the new confirmation gate. The insert-mode path does, but prompt-mode slash routing is a separate path in both `src/terminal_app.rs` and `src/ui_harness.rs`, so that needs scrutiny.
@work-leaf read src/ui.rs src/cli.rs

I requested `src/ui.rs` and `src/cli.rs` to verify whether READY is only a UI marker and whether slash commands are intentionally routed as chat messages. That should let me separate genuine regressions from expected terminal behavior.

The UI layer confirms that `READY` is entirely local (`TerminalUi::set_agent_ready_state`), while backend sends still occur through `TerminalController::send_message`. I’m checking whether the patch’s local prompt-dismissal creates stale state when the controller later emits session updates, and whether there is a missed route that bypasses dismissal.

FINDINGS

- `src/terminal_app.rs:640` bypasses the new completion-confirmation state for prompt-mode slash messages. `WorkLeafController::apply_agent_result` appends `work-leaf: is this feature done? yes/no` and selects the patch chat after `CommandChatResult::ReviewComplete` (`src/workspace.rs:685`), and insert-mode chat clears the pending confirmation through `send_chat_buffer` -> `handle_feature_done_confirmation` (`src/terminal_app.rs:667`, `src/terminal_app.rs:684`). But `route_prompt_slash_command` sends `:/...` input directly to the selected agent via `controller.send_message` without clearing `feature_done_confirmations`. A follow-up like `:/status` is a nonempty patch-agent message, so it should reopen the conversation; instead the confirmation remains pending and the next `yes` can be swallowed incorrectly. The harness mirrors the same gap in `src/ui_harness.rs:256`.

- Missing required real-agent verification for an agent-facing change. This scope changes review completion routing in `src/workspace.rs::apply_agent_result` and terminal chat behavior in `src/terminal_app.rs::send_chat_buffer`, both covered by the AGENTS.md real-agent verification rule. The provided implementation summary cites automated coverage but no exact real Codex/backend scenario or documented pre-agent blocker, so the patch is not ready under the repository rules.

@work-leaf done
I’ll re-check the updated mediated state for the files touched by the follow-up, then compare it against the two original findings: slash follow-ups and real-agent verification.
@work-leaf read src/terminal_app.rs src/ui_harness.rs tests/ui_harness.rs docs/architecture.md
I have the updated bundle. I’m checking the exact routed paths now: `route_prompt_slash_command` in both terminal adapters, the confirmation helper, and the new regression tests.

The follow-up does route `:/...` through the same confirmation gate now. I’m reading surrounding lines to make sure it still sends non-`yes` slash input normally and only swallows explicit completion confirmation.

NO_FINDINGS

Reviewed patch scope for `user-1` changes terminal UI behavior only.

It adds vim-style visual selection to the focused pane through [src/ui.rs](src/ui.rs) `TerminalUi`:

- `v` starts character visual selection.
- `V` starts line visual selection.
- `Ctrl-V` starts block visual selection.
- `h/j/k/l` and arrow keys extend the active selection.
- `y` and `Y` copy the selected text.
- `Esc` clears the active selection.

Selection is tracked by `VisualSelection`, `VisualKind`, and `VisualPosition` in `src/ui.rs`. Rendering is applied through `TerminalUi::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer`, which marks selected cells with reverse-video styling before `buffer_to_string` emits the terminal frame. Copy behavior is handled by `TerminalUi::yank_visual_selection`, `selected_visual_text`, `pending_copy_text`, and the OSC 52 clipboard prefix emitted from `render_screen_with_prompt` / `render_screen_with_cursors`.

The terminal byte adapters were updated so real terminal input can reach the shared UI logic:

- [src/terminal_app.rs](src/terminal_app.rs) maps byte `22` to `UiKey::Char('\u{16}')` for `Ctrl-V`.
- [src/ui_harness.rs](src/ui_harness.rs) does the same for harness tests.

Coverage added:

- [tests/terminal_ui.rs](tests/terminal_ui.rs) covers right-pane character selection/yank and left-pane line/block selection/yank.
- [tests/ui_harness.rs](tests/ui_harness.rs) covers harness-level `v...y` copy and `Ctrl-V` block-mode entry.

Docs updated:

- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) describes terminal visual selection as terminal frontend behavior owned by `TerminalUi`, not controller or backend behavior.

Validation completed:

- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets --all-features`
- Real Codex smoke check returned `WORK_LEAF_REAL_AGENT_OK`.

reviewer findings:
FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1009`: wrapped pane content cannot be selected or highlighted beyond the first rendered row. `right_widget` renders the focused right pane with `Paragraph::wrap(Wrap { trim: false })` at `src/ui.rs:727-735`, while visual state is built from logical newline-delimited strings via `remember_visual_lines` / `visual_lines` at `src/ui.rs:922-924` and `src/ui.rs:1696-1697`. `apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` then maps each selected logical line to a single terminal row with `y = 1 + range.row` and clamps columns to `inner_width` at `src/ui.rs:1009-1017`. For any long chat line that wraps in the terminal, `l` can move the visual cursor past the visible first row, and `y` can copy those characters through `selected_visual_text` at `src/ui.rs:901-919`, but the rendered selection never reaches the wrapped continuation rows. The user sees no highlight for text being selected/copied, so visual mode is incorrect for normal wrapped terminal output.

I’ll fix this in the terminal UI layer and add a regression test first. The failure is in `src/ui.rs::apply_visual_selection_to_buffer` using logical newline rows while `right_widget` wraps them, so the test will cover a wrapped right-pane line where copied text must also be visibly highlighted on the continuation row.

The patch makes prompt-mode slash input route to the selected agent instead of being interpreted as a Work Leaf command.

In `src/terminal_app.rs`, prompt submission now detects lines that start with `/` and whose next character is not whitespace. When a selected agent exists, `TerminalAppCore::route_prompt_slash_command` sends that raw line through `controller.send_message(&agent_id, line)`, which follows the existing path into `WorkLeafController::send_message`, `CommandChat::send_to_agent_streaming_with_ids`, and the selected backend’s `send_streaming`. That means `:/status` resumes the selected backend agent and renders its reply in that agent chat transcript.

`src/ui_harness.rs` mirrors the same behavior for terminal harness coverage. The added tests in `tests/terminal_app.rs` and `tests/ui_harness.rs` prove `:/status` becomes `user-1> /status` / `user: /status`, does not fall through as an unknown fixture command, and reaches the backend as raw `/status`.

The patch also documents the resulting terminal behavior in `docs/architecture.md`. Formatter follow-up commits only normalize formatting and add/format adjacent test coverage, including the existing terminal clipboard base64 helper coverage in `src/ui.rs`.

@work-leaf done

The reviewed scope adds review-completion confirmation behavior across the controller and terminal UI.

When `WorkLeafController` handles `CommandChatResult::ReviewComplete`, it records the review, appends the review summary to the reviewed patch agent, appends `work-leaf: is this feature done? yes/no`, and emits `WorkLeafEvent::AgentSelected` for that patch agent. That makes the patch-agent chat the active chat after review finishes.

`TerminalAppCore` tracks chats with pending feature-done prompts. If the selected chat receives `yes` or `y`, the UI clears the `READY` highlight locally and does not send anything to the backend. If the user types any other nonempty message, the confirmation state is cleared and the message is sent normally to the patch agent, reopening work on that chat.

`UiHarness` mirrors the same state transition so automated terminal UI coverage can verify it. Coverage includes controller review completion prompting/reselection, terminal-app confirmation handling, and UI-harness behavior. Documentation in `docs/architecture.md` describes the resulting workflow.
codex: reviewer findings routed back for fixes

reviewer findings:
FINDINGS

- `src/terminal_app.rs:640` bypasses the new completion-confirmation state for prompt-mode slash messages. `WorkLeafController::apply_agent_result` appends `work-leaf: is this feature done? yes/no` and selects the patch chat after `CommandChatResult::ReviewComplete` (`src/workspace.rs:685`), and insert-mode chat clears the pending confirmation through `send_chat_buffer` -> `handle_feature_done_confirmation` (`src/terminal_app.rs:667`, `src/terminal_app.rs:684`). But `route_prompt_slash_command` sends `:/...` input directly to the selected agent via `controller.send_message` without clearing `feature_done_confirmations`. A follow-up like `:/status` is a nonempty patch-agent message, so it should reopen the conversation; instead the confirmation remains pending and the next `yes` can be swallowed incorrectly. The harness mirrors the same gap in `src/ui_harness.rs:256`.

- Missing required real-agent verification for an agent-facing change. This scope changes review completion routing in `src/workspace.rs::apply_agent_result` and terminal chat behavior in `src/terminal_app.rs::send_chat_buffer`, both covered by the AGENTS.md real-agent verification rule. The provided implementation summary cites automated coverage but no exact real Codex/backend scenario or documented pre-agent blocker, so the patch is not ready under the repository rules.

@work-leaf done

I’ll fix the confirmation state on the prompt-mode slash route that bypassed normal chat submission. I’ll add coverage that a `/status` follow-up clears the pending confirmation and is still sent, so a later `yes` is treated as a normal message instead of being swallowed.
The fix is applied. I’m running the two confirmation-specific tests first, then the required format, clippy, full test, and real-agent smoke check again.

The new regression coverage is green: prompt-mode `/status` now clears the confirmation and later `yes` is sent normally. I’m running `cargo fmt --all --check` next.
Formatting is clean. Next is clippy with all targets/features and warnings denied.
@work-leaf locks run target -- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
Clippy is clean. I’m running the full test suite again before the final real-agent smoke check.
All required automated checks are green after the fix. I’m running the real Codex smoke check again and will report the exact scenario/result.
